Title: The foxtail millet (Setaria italica) terpene synthase gene family

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal activity against Fusarium verticillioides and Fusarium subglutinans; likely disease defense/stress resilience
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes C19 hydroxylation of SiTPS8-derived labdane diterpene scaffolds, producing diterpene alcohols including abietadien-19-ol and syn-pimara-7,15-dien-19-ol; these products were detected in planta.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated P450 (native protein inferred from ER membrane anchor; truncated for E. coli assays)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SiTPS8 and SiTPS9; induced by mycoprotein treatment in stems
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected as inducible transcript under pathogen-mimic mycoprotein treatment; co-expression with SiTPS8/SiTPS9
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mycoprotein from Fusarium venenatum; oxidative stress (CuSO4); drought; UV irradiation; pathogen-related elicitation context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli combinatorial co-expression with SiTPS8 and class II diTPS partners; GC-MS; product purification; NMR; in planta LC-MS/MS detection; antifungal bioassays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP99A17 was identified by co-expression/genomic proximity with SiTPS8/SiTPS9. 2. It oxidized multiple SiTPS8-derived diterpene olefins to hydroxylated products. 3. NMR verified abietadien-19-ol and syn-pimara-7,15-dien-19-ol as products. 4. These diterpene alcohols accumulated in S. italica tissues and showed antifungal activity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering pathogen-defense diterpenoid branches in millet or other cereals; could be stacked with SiTPS8 and appropriate CPP synthases to generate antifungal diterpene alcohols.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth/yield penalty reported; in planta metabolite abundance was low and pathway may require additional downstream tailoring enzymes.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Publicly available co-expression/transcript datasets from Phytozome/Phytomine; targeted LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; qPCR expression analysis; accession NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP99A19 was co-expressed but inactive in tested assays. CYP99A17 differed from rice CYP99A2/A3 by catalyzing hydroxylation to alcohols rather than full oxidation to acids.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene synthase; TPS-c clade; DxDD motif
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms central precursor pool for specialized diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the syn-CPP precursor for downstream specialized diterpenoid biosynthesis in S. italica.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: qPCR showed stress responsiveness under tested abiotic/biotic treatments
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: oxidative stress (CuSO4); drought; UV irradiation; mycoprotein elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli co-expression assay; comparison with OsCPS4 product by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS6 was biochemically identified as a syn-CPP synthase. 2. The enzyme expands the stereochemical range of class II diTPSs in foxtail millet. 3. It likely feeds specialized diterpenoid branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supplying gene for engineering syn-CPP-derived diterpenoid defense metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR; public transcript data; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No detectable product in N. benthamiana; activity observed in E. coli.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase; TPS-e/f-related; bi-domain architecture
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ent-, (+)-, or syn-CPP into multiple diterpene olefins including ent-pimara-8,15-diene, ent-pimara-8(14),15-diene, ent-sandaracopimaradiene, ent-abietadiene, sandaracopimaradiene, abietadiene, syn-pimara-7,15-diene and syn-pimara-9(11),15-diene; also converts FPP to trans-nerolidol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms scaffold precursors of diterpene alcohol defense metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch enzyme, not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: abietadien-19-ol and/or syn-pimara-7,15-dien-19-ol detected in leaves; stems; roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates multiple diterpene scaffolds from all three CPP stereoisomers and thereby underpins diverse specialized diterpenoid metabolism in S. italica.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant in aerial organs; induced by mycoprotein treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stress-responsive transcript, especially under mycoprotein treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mycoprotein elicitation; oxidative stress; drought; UV irradiation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli and N. benthamiana combinatorial expression assays; GC-MS; product purification; NMR; co-expression with CYP99A17; in planta metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS8 is a highly promiscuous class I diTPS accepting ent-, (+)- and syn-CPP. 2. It forms multiple pimaradiene, sandaracopimaradiene and abietadiene scaffolds. 3. NMR confirmed ent-pimara-8,15-diene and syn-pimara-7,15-diene among products. 4. Together with CYP99A17 it produces antifungal diterpene alcohol branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value scaffold gene for metabolic engineering of diverse cereal diterpenoid defenses; can be stacked with specific CPP synthases and CYP99A17.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may cause metabolic branching/off-target flux; no growth penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public co-expression data; qPCR; LC-MS/MS; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bi-domain architecture is unusual for this class; no gene synteny across analyzed Poaceae genomes was noted.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene synthase; TPS-c clade; specialized-metabolism-related
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms central precursor pool for specialized diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides (+)-CPP precursor for specialized diterpenoid branches utilized by SiTPS8.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant in aerial organs; induced by mycoprotein treatment; co-expressed with SiTPS8 and CYP99A17
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mycoprotein elicitation; oxidative stress; drought; UV irradiation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana combinatorial expression with MvELS/GrEKS stereochemical validation; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS9 was verified as a (+)-CPP synthase. 2. It adds unusual class II stereochemical diversity to foxtail millet. 3. It is co-expressed with SiTPS8 and CYP99A17, supporting a specialized diterpenoid pathway role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor gene for engineering (+)-CPP-derived diterpenoid branches in cereals.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public co-expression data; qPCR; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike other grasses, S. italica contains enzymes producing ent-, (+)- and syn-CPP.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: possible biotic and abiotic stress response; weak antifungal activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces α-bisabolol as a stress-responsive sesquiterpenoid product.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant in panicles; induced by drought stress and mycoprotein treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana expression; GC-MS comparison with standard; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS15 encodes an α-bisabolol synthase. 2. Transcript abundance increases under drought and fungal elicitor treatment. 3. The product showed only low antifungal efficacy in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/gene for stress-responsive volatile or semi-volatile terpenoid pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weak antifungal effect suggests limited standalone deployment value.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR; public transcript data; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Syntenic conservation lacked ortholog in Sorghum bicolor according to discussion.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: possible defense-related sesquiterpenoid; weak antifungal activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces δ-cadinene and is the most broadly stress-induced TPS transcript among those tested.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher abundance in leaf and panicle; induced by all stresses except UV
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad stress inducibility in roots/stems and aerial tissues depending on treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: oxidative stress; drought; mycoprotein treatment; not UV-induced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana expression; GC-MS comparison with standard; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS19 encodes a δ-cadinene synthase. 2. It was the only transcript significantly induced by nearly all tested stresses except UV. 3. This suggests a broad defense-related role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential broad stress-inducible defense pathway gene for breeding markers or engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product had only limited antifungal activity in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR; public transcript data; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytically redundant with SiTPS27 but differently regulated.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: associated with herbivore/defense responses in grasses; weak antifungal activity in vitro here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces β-caryophyllene; transcript induced by mycoprotein treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant in aerial organs; induced by mycoprotein treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana expression; GC-MS comparison with standard; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS20 encodes a β-caryophyllene synthase. 2. Expression is induced by fungal elicitor treatment. 3. The product showed only modest antifungal activity in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering volatile defense signaling or herbivore-associated traits.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited direct antifungal efficacy in assay.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR; public transcript data; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Consistent with β-caryophyllene occurrence in rice and maize defense systems.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: possible stress response metabolite; bioactivity unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces cubebol in E. coli assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by drought stress and mycoprotein treatment according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli co-expression with ZmFPPS; GC-MS comparison with cubebol synthase product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS21 was identified as a cubebol synthase. 2. The gene is stress responsive. 3. Purified cubebol was unstable during antifungal assay conditions and not tested further.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential source of unusual sesquiterpenoid chemistry for trait engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product instability complicates functional deployment and phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected activity only in E. coli, not N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No substantial changes under tested stressors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: tested under oxidative stress; drought; UV; mycoprotein
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli co-expression with ZmFPPS; GC-MS; product purification; 2D NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS25 encodes an eudesme-2,11-diol synthase. 2. Product identity was NMR-verified. 3. Unlike the analogous maize pathway, the metabolite was not detected in S. italica tissues under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables engineering of rare sesquiterpene alcohol traits, but biological function remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No in planta accumulation detected under tested conditions; utility uncertain.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents syntenic ortholog of maize ZmTPS16 according to discussion.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces germacrene D and is strongly induced by oxidative stress.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant in leaves; highest transcript changes under oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana expression; GC-MS comparison with standard; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS26 encodes a germacrene D synthase. 2. It shows strong oxidative stress inducibility. 3. It likely contributes to stress-responsive sesquiterpenoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker or engineering target for oxidative-stress-associated terpenoid responses.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct bioactivity established in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces δ-cadinene with a regulatory pattern distinct from SiTPS19.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most significantly increased in UV-stressed leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana expression; GC-MS comparison with standard; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS27 encodes a δ-cadinene synthase. 2. It is catalytically redundant with SiTPS19. 3. Its transcript is preferentially induced by UV stress, indicating condition-specific regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering UV-responsive terpenoid defense.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytically redundant with SiTPS19 but differentially regulated.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase; TPS-e/f clade
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ent-CPP to ent-kaurene; also converts FPP to trans-nerolidol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms ent-kaurene precursor or diverts flux to trans-nerolidol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: GA precursor and possible specialized metabolism contribution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as ent-kaurene synthase and also shows dual sesquiterpene synthase activity forming trans-nerolidol.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased transcript abundance under UV irradiation
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: plastid for diterpene activity; possible altered compartment use for dual activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana co-expression with ZmAn2/IrTPS3; E. coli co-expression with ZmFPPS; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS28 was verified as an ent-kaurene synthase. 2. It also converts FPP to trans-nerolidol, indicating dual activity. 3. Combined evidence suggests a likely role in specialized diterpenoid metabolism rather than core GA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially useful for engineering ent-kaurene-derived defense pathways, but dual activity should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dual activity may divert flux; exact separation from GA metabolism unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests SiTPS35/SiTPS28 pair may function in specialized metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase; TPS-e/f clade
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal step in GA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene from ent-CPP, likely serving canonical GA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana co-expression with ZmAn2/IrTPS3; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS29 was verified as an ent-kaurene synthase. 2. Synteny with canonical ent-CPP/ent-kaurene synthase pairs supports a GA-biosynthetic role. 3. It contrasts with the more specialized SiTPS28 branch.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily relevant as a reference/core hormone-pathway enzyme; manipulation may impact GA biology.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential hormone/growth effects if perturbed, though not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests SiTPS34/SiTPS29 function in GA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene synthase; TPS-c clade; His-Asn dyad-containing ent-CPP synthase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for ent-kaurene and GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP and likely functions in canonical GA biosynthesis together with SiTPS29.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not induced by drought in contrast to SiTPS35
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought context used to distinguish from SiTPS35
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana co-expression assays with GrEKS/MvELS stereochemical validation; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS34 was verified as an ent-CPP synthase. 2. Phylogeny and synteny support a role in GA biosynthesis. 3. It is distinct from the stress-inducible specialized ent-CPP synthase SiTPS35.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Generally not a preferred engineering target for defense traits because of likely core hormone function.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely growth/hormone tradeoffs if altered, inferred from probable GA role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Placed in conserved syntenic network with OsCPS1, ZmAN1 and PvCPS14/15.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene synthase; TPS-c clade; ent-CPP synthase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool for specialized diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP and is stress inducible, suggesting a role in specialized diterpenoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana co-expression assays with GrEKS/MvELS stereochemical validation; GC-MS; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS35 was verified as an ent-CPP synthase. 2. Unlike SiTPS34, it is drought inducible. 3. Combined phylogenetic and expression evidence suggests a specialized metabolism role with SiTPS28.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: More attractive than SiTPS34 for engineering defense diterpenoid branches because it likely operates in specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion distinguishes it from the probable GA-biosynthetic SiTPS34.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I sesquiterpene synthase; TPS-a2 clade
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces germacrene-D-4-ol, which was detected in planta specifically in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inducible in roots exposed to drought according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana expression; GC-MS comparison with authentic enzymatic product; LC-MS/MS detection in plant tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiTPS38 encodes a germacrene-D-4-ol synthase. 2. Germacrene-D-4-ol was detected in S. italica roots. 3. Expression patterns suggest a role in below-ground stress responses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for root-associated terpenoid defense engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR/public transcript data; LC-MS/MS; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Switchgrass syntenic ortholog produces related germacrene D according to discussion.
